use_cases.audience: Engineers and platform teams running coding agents that can use OpenAI-compatible chat completions.
use_cases.demo_prompt: Audit this repository for failing tests, propose the smallest fix, and run the relevant test command. Use Chutes for model calls and keep credentials in CHUTES_API_KEY.
proof_points: Chutes exposes OpenAI-compatible chat completions at https://llm.chutes.ai/v1.
Standard Bearer authorization with CHUTES_API_KEY was re-verified on the inference and management hosts on 2026-06-11.
The live /v1/models endpoint is public and is the source of truth for model IDs, pricing, context windows, capabilities, and confidential_compute.
use_cases.setup_path: Set CHUTES_API_KEY in the agent runtime, point the OpenAI-compatible base URL at https://llm.chutes.ai/v1, and choose a live model ID from /v1/models (or a routing alias such as default:latency after configuring a pool once at chutes.ai/app -> Model Routing).
use_cases.title: Run Codex on Chutes
use_cases.value_prop: Give Codex-style coding agents open-source, TEE-backed inference through the OpenAI API shape they already understand.

use_cases.audience: Claude Code, Claude Cowork, and plugin-marketplace users who want Chutes skills inside their assistant workflow.
use_cases.demo_prompt: Install the Chutes skill suite, list available TEE-backed models, and recommend a routing strategy for a private coding assistant.
proof_points: The plugin tree contains Chutes skills for account setup, inference, routing, usage, platform ops, MCP portability, and TEE evidence parsing.
Read-only Chutes skill paths were re-verified against the live API on 2026-06-11.
Credential handling is backed by manage_credentials.py so agents do not need to paste API keys into chat.
use_cases.setup_path: Add this repo as a plugin marketplace or copy plugins/chutes-ai/skills into the active Claude skills directory.
use_cases.title: Use Chutes from Claude skills
use_cases.value_prop: Turn Chutes operations into agent-callable skills while keeping endpoint facts and credential handling centralized.

use_cases.audience: Hermes users who want Chutes as a named OpenAI-compatible provider and optional MCP tool surface.
use_cases.demo_prompt: Configure Hermes with Chutes as a custom provider, use default:latency for chat, and mount the Chutes MCP server for account read tools.
proof_points: The repo ships a Hermes skill mirror under other-agents/hermes/skills.
Hermes config examples cover basic provider setup, cheap routing, delegation, and the research endpoint.
The guide distinguishes current Hermes custom-provider support from unsupported first-class-provider claims.
use_cases.setup_path: Store CHUTES_API_KEY in the Hermes environment, add a providers.chutes entry with base_url https://llm.chutes.ai/v1, and optionally register the local Chutes MCP server.
use_cases.title: Run Hermes on Chutes
use_cases.value_prop: Use Chutes for Hermes chat, delegation, background work, and account-aware tools without waiting for a built-in provider.

use_cases.agent: Cursor, Cline, Aider, and MCP clients
use_cases.audience: Developers who want Chutes available in editor agents and local coding tools.
use_cases.demo_prompt: Generate a Cursor MCP config for Chutes, run the MCP server self-check, and explain which tools are read-only versus beta write paths.
proof_points: The chutes-mcp-portability skill can generate configs for Cursor, Aider, Hermes, and related OpenAI-compatible clients.
The MCP server exposes model discovery, quota, aliases, usage, discounts, chutes, and API key read tools.
Write and deploy MCP tools remain beta because they can mutate account or compute state.
use_cases.setup_path: Run generate_agent_config.py for the target client, keep CHUTES_API_KEY in the environment or keychain, and run chutes-mcp-server --self-check.
use_cases.title: Bring Chutes to editor agents with MCP
use_cases.value_prop: Make Chutes discoverable and callable inside local coding agents while keeping secrets outside checked-in config.

use_cases.audience: Teams routing chat apps and channel agents through the OpenClaw gateway.
use_cases.demo_prompt: Add Chutes as an OpenClaw provider, configure a latency routing pool for channel agents, and warn when the research endpoint is not appropriate.
proof_points: Chutes fits OpenClaw as an OpenAI-compatible provider block.
The Chutes side of the integration is live-verified, while OpenClaw field names are marked doc-derived until an end-to-end install is exercised.
Routing suffixes such as :latency and :throughput can be represented as model IDs when the client passes IDs through.
use_cases.setup_path: Export CHUTES_API_KEY, add a chutes provider under models.providers with baseUrl https://llm.chutes.ai/v1, and select either a live model ID or routing pool.
use_cases.title: Run channel agents on Chutes with OpenClaw
use_cases.value_prop: Put open-source TEE inference behind Discord, Slack, Matrix, and other channel agents through a self-hosted gateway.

use_cases.audience: Builders using OpenAI SDKs, LangChain, LiteLLM, Vercel AI SDK, AutoGPT-style tools, or custom agent loops.
use_cases.demo_prompt: Port this OpenAI SDK agent to Chutes by changing only base URL, API key environment variable, and model selection from the live catalog.
proof_points: Standard OpenAI SDKs send Authorization: Bearer, which is the live-verified Chutes auth shape.
Model capabilities such as tools, JSON mode, structured outputs, modalities, and pricing are exposed in /v1/models.
Routing aliases let callers avoid hardcoding one model for uptime-sensitive agents.
use_cases.setup_path: Set the SDK base URL to https://llm.chutes.ai/v1, read CHUTES_API_KEY from the environment, and select a live model ID or routing alias.
use_cases.title: Use one OpenAI-compatible endpoint for many agent stacks
use_cases.value_prop: Reuse existing OpenAI-compatible client code while moving inference to Chutes-hosted open-source models.
